name: Diagnostic / troubleshooting (first hour)
description: On-site electrical troubleshooting: systematic diagnosis of the reported problem with findings explained and a repair quote provided.
labor_notes: First hour covers arrival, interview, and systematic testing; most single-symptom issues are identified within it.

name: Diagnostic / troubleshooting (each additional hour)
description: Continued troubleshooting beyond the first hour for complex or intermittent problems.
labor_notes: Billed per additional hour; intermittent faults and multi-circuit interactions are the usual reason to go past hour one.

name: Breaker trip diagnosis
description: Diagnose a repeatedly tripping breaker: isolate the fault to device, wiring, or load, with a written finding.
labor_notes: Splitting the circuit and load-testing sections typically brackets the fault within 90 minutes; repair is quoted separately.

name: Dead outlet / dead circuit trace
description: Trace and identify the cause of dead receptacles or a dead circuit section.
labor_notes: Most dead-outlet calls resolve to a failed device, tripped GFCI, or open splice found by methodical tracing; includes minor re-termination.

name: Whole-home electrical inspection
description: Room-by-room electrical inspection: panel condition, GFCI/AFCI coverage, device sampling, visible wiring, and a written prioritized report.
labor_notes: A thorough walk of a typical single-family home with panel dead-front off, device sampling, and report write-up runs about three hours.

name: Panel tune-up (torque, thermal check, label)
description: Preventive panel service: re-torque terminations to spec, thermal scan under load, cleanup, and a fresh circuit directory.
labor_notes: A disciplined pass over every termination plus labeling; catches loose lugs before they become burn-ups.

name: Insurance / 4-point electrical report
description: Electrical inspection and written report formatted for insurance requirements (panel brand/condition, wiring types, protection coverage).
labor_notes: Insurer checklists require specific photos and declarations; two hours covers inspection plus the formatted report.

name: Flickering / dimming lights diagnosis
description: Diagnose whole-home or circuit-level flickering and dimming, including service connection and neutral integrity checks.
labor_notes: Flicker complaints require monitoring under load steps and checking service terminations — utility-side findings are documented for referral.

name: GFCI nuisance trip diagnosis
description: Diagnose repeated GFCI tripping: isolate leakage source across downstream devices, appliances, or wiring.
labor_notes: Downstream splitting with a leakage tester usually isolates the culprit appliance or damp box within the allotted time.

name: Pre-purchase electrical walkthrough
description: Electrical walkthrough during a home-purchase window with a same-day verbal summary and follow-up written estimate of needed corrections.
labor_notes: Buyers need fast turnaround: a focused two-hour assessment of panel, wiring era, and safety items, with repair pricing to follow.
